content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA—HEALTH AND WELFARE AGENCY

EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

DEPARTMENT OF BENEFIT PAYMENTS
1144 P Street
Sacramento, CA 95814
Tele: 916/322-5603

May 31, 1977


KENNETH GOSHORN, INC.
Fair Political Practices
Commission
1100 K Street
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814


Dear Mr. Goshorn:

This letter is a request for advice on the reporting requirements
of designated employees of this department who must report investments in business entities and income from sources which are
subject to audit by the Department of Benefit Payments. As you
know, this department audit California employers and collects
from them not only unemployment and Disability Insurance payments
but also personal income tax withholding. Specifically we need
your advice in regard to employee investment in and income from
the State of California Deferred Compensation Plan for Employees.

Enclosed is a brochure, The Deferred Compensation Plan for
Employees Of The State Of California, which explains the workings
of the California plan.

It is our opinion that at least participation through Great
Western Savings and Loan Association is exempt from reporting
requirements. It should also be noted that even though the
individual chooses the type of investment made, the State holds
title to the funds and has some discretion in investment and
repayment.

I am of course available to assist you by further explaining the
duties of our designated employees.

Sincerely,

James D. Shimon
JAMES D. SHIMON
Attorney

Enclosure

James D. Simon
June 27, 1977
page two

intended or necessary under the Political Reform Act.

Thank you for writing. I trust this answers your question.
If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to call on
me at 2-6441.

Sincerely,

Kenneth W. Goshorn
Research Specialist III
Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa
Attachment

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 27, 1977

James D. Simon
Staff Attorney
Department of Benefit Payments
744 P Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Simon:

This is in response to your letter of 31 May requesting informal advice concerning whether under the Department's Conflict of Interest Code designated employees are required to report as an "investment" or a "source of income", contributions to the State of California Deferred Compensation Plan for employees.

Our advice is that they need not be reported. Having analyzed the descriptive materials of the plan, we cannot conclude that any of the various insurance plans are "investments". Article X of the plan clearly states:

"The State shall have the sole ownership of all investments made pursuant to this Plan and no participant shall have any interests therein or the right to acquire the same."

Furthermore, Government Code §82034 excludes "any insurance policy" and seems to require an ownership interest of some nature in order to constitute an "investment". Similarly, §82030 which defines "income" excludes from that definition both "salary from a state agency [§82030(b)(1)] and "any insurance policy" and interest or deposits in financial institutions and shares in a credit union [§2030(b)(7)]. We think the deferred salary plan, taken as a whole, is "salary from a state agency" within the meaning of §82030(b)(2). We believe that the policy enunciated in its formal opinion No. 76-074, Richard J. Moore, supports this conclusion. We do not believe that reporting (and potential disqualification) by designated employees of their participation in this plan is

2.37

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION 916 327 5660 · EXECUTIVE LEGAL 322 5901 · ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322 2641

As you know, this department audits all California employers and collects from them not only Unemployment and Disability Insurance payments but also personal income tax withholding. Specifically we need your advice in regard to employee investment in and income from the State of California Deferred Compensation Plan for Employees.
